signals.title: NZD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/JPY at 114.056 — NZD outperformance dominates G8 peers as RBNZ maintains a hawkish stance vs BoJ. NZD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading the CB) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the next meeting. If RBNZ signals further tightening, a crowded spec short unwind targets the 115.00 handle. JPY remains the G8 laggard despite positive economic surprises (CESI +11), as the -150bp NZD-JPY carry advantage persists.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7840 — IV at 19.5% vs HV30 at 6.1% creates a massive +13.4% VRP, signaling extreme institutional hedging demand for CHF strength. This institutional fear diverges from retail sentiment where 76% of traders are long USD/CHF. Given the 375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage, retail is chasing the yield while the options market pays a record premium for downside protection. Watch for a break of 0.7800 to trigger retail capitulation.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7164 — COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long) represents a crowded institutional position. While the 72bp AUD carry premium (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.63%) supports the pair, the elevated VRP of +5.5% (IV 13.5% vs HV30 8.0%) and a -0.71 risk reversal skew indicate the options market is aggressively hedging against a long-unwind. Weak US GDP and Iran deal hopes provide a temporary tailwind, but positioning remains at risk of a correction if RBA shifts to neutral.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.275 — The 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) keeps the pair bid despite Fed's Musalem signaling a shift away from easing. 160.00 remains the critical level for BoJ verbal warnings and potential intervention. Options market shows moderate caution with a -1.11 25d RR put skew, suggesting traders are paying for protection against a sudden JPY rally despite the carry-driven uptrend. Carry dominates while VIX remains at 15.6 (normal volatility).
